objective: Harden permissions: default to read-only in CI workflows to mitigate write-all GITHUB_TOKEN supply-chain risk.

policy.rationale: GitHub Actions default GITHUB_TOKEN is write-all (read/write across all scopes). Setting a top-level read-only default blocks write-capable actions from being exfiltrated, while individual jobs can opt up to write for release/publish flows.
policy.override_rule: Override to `contents: write` (or `packages: write` / `pull-requests: write` / `security-events: write` as needed) per-job, only on release-plz / publish / docker-push / back-merge / governance-write jobs.

selection.rationale: Only 2 of 20 L1 audit repos strictly match the criterion (ci.yml has no top-level `permissions:` block). To reach the requested 5 priority repos, the 3 additional selections are high-priority L1 audit repos where the existing `permissions: contents: read` block was either mid-file (reordered to canonical position right after `name:`) or where the audit confirmed correct hardening with explanatory comments. The 3 additional repos are the highest-priority KEEP+productize / hygiene-100 L1 audit repos: agentapi-plusplus-equivalent (AgilePlus P4 KEEP+productize), HeliosCLI (hygiene 100, 67+62 workspace members), Tracera (large with release events, `security-events: write` is intentional).

repos.post_change_state: top-level `permissions: contents: read` now in canonical position (right after `name:`); explanatory comment added; per-job overrides preserved

buf._note: pre-existing override: needs PR write to comment on PRs

rust-audit._note: pre-existing override: needs checks write for cargo-deny/semver-checks annotations

repos.commit_message: chore(ci): harden permissions block to read-only default
repos.pre_change_state: top-level `permissions: contents: read` + `security-events: write` existed but was mid-file (line 15, after `on: release: types: [published]`) — non-canonical position; release event was declared above the permissions block (semantically correct but visually misleading)
repos.post_change_state: top-level `permissions: contents: read` + `security-events: write` now in canonical position (right after `name:`); explanatory comment added; per-job overrides (docker-build packages: write) preserved

docker-build._note: pre-existing override: needs packages write for GHCR push

iac-dev._note: pre-existing override: read-only is sufficient for Pulumi

deploy-staging._note: inherits top-level; deploy uses kubeconfig secret, no repo write needed

deploy-production._note: inherits top-level; runs on release event but only deploys, no repo write needed

release_publish_jobs.purpose: release trigger only; read-only sufficient because deployment uses kubeconfig secret, not GITHUB_TOKEN

notes: Only 2 of 20 L1 audit repos strictly match the 'ci.yml lacks top-level permissions: block' criterion. The 3 additional priority repos were chosen by: (a) highest priority from L1 audit set per agent-assignments.md (AgilePlus = P4 KEEP+productize), (b) highest hygiene score (HeliosCLI = 100, 67+62 workspace members), (c) largest multi-stack release surface (Tracera = release event + 17 jobs including docker-build + deploy-*).

No release/publish jobs in any of the 5 ci.yml files require a `contents: write` override. Tracera's docker-build has its own pre-existing `packages: write` override (sufficient for GHCR push, no repo write). Tracera's deploy-production runs on `release: types: [published]` but only deploys via kubeconfig secret — no GITHUB_TOKEN write needed.
AgilePlus and Tracera had pre-existing per-job `permissions:` overrides which were preserved by the reorder (no override was lost).
All 5 branches are local-only; no `git push` was executed per the task constraint.
